qa_pairs.question: What is the parent page for this document?
qa_pairs.answer: The parent page for this document is Cloud.

qa_pairs.question: What is a mandatory step before accessing a cloud?
qa_pairs.answer: You cannot access a cloud without first having a cloud project.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find instructions on obtaining a cloud project?
qa_pairs.answer: To get a cloud project, you can refer to the 'Getting a cloud project' section on the Cloud page.

qa_pairs.question: What important item will I receive after a cloud project is associated with my account?
qa_pairs.answer: You will receive a confirmation email with important details needed to access your project and get started with the cloud.

qa_pairs.question: Which web browsers are recommended for the cloud project's web interface?
qa_pairs.answer: Firefox and Chrome web browsers are recommended for accessing the cloud project's web interface.

qa_pairs.question: What error message indicates that my browser might not be supported?
qa_pairs.answer: The error message 'Danger: There was an error submitting the form. Please try again.' suggests that your browser is not supported.

qa_pairs.question: If I encounter browser issues, who should I contact?
qa_pairs.answer: If you are still having issues with browser compatibility, you should email technical support.

qa_pairs.question: What are virtual machines also known as?
qa_pairs.answer: Virtual machines are also referred to as instances or VMs.

qa_pairs.question: How can I access virtual machines stored on the cloud?
qa_pairs.answer: You can access virtual machines from your personal computer using our web interface.

qa_pairs.question: How do I find the login link for my cloud project?
qa_pairs.answer: The confirmation email you received includes a link to the cloud interface your project is associated with.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if my default web browser is not compatible for logging into the cloud interface?
qa_pairs.answer: If your default web browser is not compatible, open a compatible web browser and copy and paste the link address into it.

qa_pairs.question: What credentials are required to log in to the cloud interface?
qa_pairs.answer: You need to use your username (not your email address) and password to log in.

qa_pairs.question: What is the name of the cloud interface platform?
qa_pairs.answer: The cloud interface platform is called OpenStack.

qa_pairs.question: What information is displayed on the OpenStack dashboard?
qa_pairs.answer: The OpenStack dashboard shows an overview of all the resources available in your project.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I learn more about navigating the OpenStack dashboard?
qa_pairs.answer: You can read the official OpenStack documentation to learn more about navigating and understanding your OpenStack dashboard.

qa_pairs.question: What types of operating systems can I use for my virtual machine?
qa_pairs.answer: You can use either a Windows operating system or a Linux operating system for your virtual machine.

qa_pairs.question: What is the distinction between the VM's operating system and my physical computer's operating system?
qa_pairs.answer: The operating system chosen is for the virtual machine or instance you are creating, not for the physical computer you are using to connect.

qa_pairs.question: Who should I contact if I'm unsure about which operating system to use for my VM?
qa_pairs.answer: If you are unsure whether you will be using Linux or Windows for your VM operating system, you should email technical support.

qa_pairs.question: Why is password authentication disabled for new virtual machines?
qa_pairs.answer: Password authentication is disabled for security reasons when you create a virtual machine.

qa_pairs.question: How can I log in to a VM created by OpenStack?
qa_pairs.answer: OpenStack creates your VM with one SSH (Secure Shell) public key installed, and you can only log in using this SSH key pair.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I already have an SSH key pair?
qa_pairs.answer: If you already have an SSH key pair, you should follow the instructions for importing an existing key pair.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I don't have an SSH key pair?
qa_pairs.answer: If you have not used SSH key pairs before or don't currently have a pair you want to use, you will need to create a key pair.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find instructions for generating SSH keys on a Windows machine?
qa_pairs.answer: You can find instructions on the 'Generating SSH keys in Windows' page.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find instructions for generating SSH keys on a Linux or Mac machine?
qa_pairs.answer: You can find instructions by following the 'Linux/Mac instructions' for using SSH keys.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find additional information on creating and managing SSH key pairs?
qa_pairs.answer: For more information on creating and managing your key pairs, see the 'SSH Keys' page in our wiki.
